disambiguation.description: The user wants to access or manage remote files via SSH, FTP, or SFTP. All file operations (list, read, write, delete, mkdir, search) work on all protocols.
examples: Show me the files on my remote server
Connect to my SSH server and list files
Read a file from the SFTP server
Upload a file to my FTP server
Delete files and folders on my server
Create a new directory on the remote server

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_listFiles
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists files and directories on a remote server connected via SSH, SFTP, or FTP. Returns file/directory names, types (file/directory/symlink), sizes in bytes, and modification timestamps. Use this to explore the remote file system structure.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The absolute remote directory path to list. Defaults to the connection's root path.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_readFile
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Reads the content of a file on a remote server. Supports four modes:

**Search within file (PREFERRED for large files):** Provide `search` to find all occurrences of a text/regex pattern within the file. Returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context lines around each match. Use this instead of reading entire large files when looking for specific code. On SSH, runs grep server-side — only matches are transferred, not the whole file.

**Line range:** Provide `startLine` and/or `endLine` for partial reading. On SSH only the requested lines are transferred.

**Tail (last N lines):** Provide `tail` to read the last N lines. Useful for logs, appending context, or seeing end of file. On SSH uses tail server-side.

**Full read:** Provide only `path` to read the entire file. Avoid for files over 500 lines — use search or line range instead.

All modes return line numbers and total line count to help plan further reads or writes.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The absolute remote file path to read.

startLine.description: Optional 1-based start line for partial reading. Cannot be combined with search or tail.

endLine.description: Optional 1-based end line for partial reading (inclusive). Cannot be combined with search or tail.

search.description: Text or regex pattern to search for within the file. Returns only matching lines with line numbers and context. Use this to navigate large files efficiently instead of multiple small reads. Cannot be combined with startLine/endLine or tail.

contextLines.description: Number of context lines to show around each search match. Default: 3. Only used with search.

tail.description: Read the last N lines of the file. Useful for logs or seeing end of file. Cannot be combined with startLine/endLine or search.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_writeFile
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Writes content to a file on a remote server. Supports seven modes:

**PREFERRED — Search/replace (use this when editing existing files):** Provide `search` and `content` to replace the first occurrence of the search text with `content`. No need to know line numbers. Supports multi-line search strings. Returns the lines around the edit so you can verify the result — no need to call readFile afterwards.

**Replace all occurrences:** Provide `search`, `content`, and `replaceAll: true` to replace ALL occurrences of the search text in the file at once. Returns count of replacements made.

**Insert before/after text:** Provide `search`, `content`, and `insertPosition` ('before' or 'after') to insert content before or after the search text WITHOUT removing it. The original text is preserved. Use this to add imports, new CSS rules, or code blocks relative to existing content.

**Append to end of file:** Use `mode='append'` to add content at the end of the file without reading it first. On SSH, appends server-side without transferring the existing content. Ideal for adding new entries, CSS rules at the end, log entries, or configuration blocks.

**Partial replace:** Provide `content` with `startLine` and `endLine` to replace only that line range.

**Insert at line:** Provide `content` with `startLine` and `mode='insert'` to insert new lines AFTER startLine.

**Full write:** Provide only `content` to create or overwrite the entire file.

Works with all protocols: SSH, SFTP, and FTP. Creates parent directories automatically.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The absolute remote file path to write to.

content.description: The content to write. For search/replace this is the replacement text. For append this is added at the end. For insert position this is inserted before/after the search text. For full writes this becomes the entire file.

search.description: Text to find in the file. When provided with content, performs find/replace. When combined with insertPosition, inserts content before/after this text without removing it. Supports multi-line strings. This is the preferred way to edit existing files — no line numbers needed.

startLine.description: Optional 1-based start line for partial write or insert.

endLine.description: Optional 1-based end line (inclusive) for partial write.

mode.description: Write mode. 'replace' (default) replaces matched text or line range. 'insert' inserts content AFTER startLine. 'append' adds content at the end of the file without reading it first.

insertPosition.description: When used with search, inserts content BEFORE or AFTER the search text without removing it. The original search text is preserved. Use this to add new code relative to existing content.

replaceAll.description: When true and search is provided, replaces ALL occurrences of search text in the file, not just the first one. Returns count of replacements made.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_searchFiles
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Searches for text patterns across multiple files on a remote server. Uses grep over SSH (skips binary files), or scans file contents for FTP. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Supports regex, fileGlob filtering, contextLines, case-sensitivity, and maxResults.

To search within a SINGLE file, use readFile with `search` instead.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The remote directory path to search in.

pattern.description: The text or regex pattern to search for.

fileGlob.description: Optional glob pattern to filter files (e.g., '*.ts', '*.py').

contextLines.description: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Default: 0 (no context). Use this when you need to understand the surrounding code of each match.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return. Default: 100.

caseSensitive.description: Whether the search is case-sensitive. Default: false (case-insensitive).

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_runCommand
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a shell command on a remote server via SSH. Returns stdout and stderr. Only for SSH/SFTP connections. Use only for tasks without a dedicated tool (builds, tests, service control, package management, custom scripts).

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

command.description: The shell command to execute on the remote server.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_deleteFile
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Deletes a file or directory on a remote server. Works with all protocols. Supports recursive deletion for directories.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The absolute remote path of the file or directory to delete.

recursive.description: Whether to recursively delete directory contents. Defaults to true.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_createDirectory
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a directory on a remote server. Works with all protocols. Automatically creates parent directories.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The absolute remote directory path to create.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_mysqlQuery
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a read-only SQL query (SELECT, SHOW, DESCRIBE, EXPLAIN) on a MySQL/MariaDB database via SSH. Returns formatted table with column headers and row count. Auto-adds LIMIT to prevent excessive output. Only works on SSH/SFTP connections.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

query.description: The SQL query to execute (SELECT, SHOW, DESCRIBE, EXPLAIN).

database.description: Optional database name to use. If omitted, no database is selected by default.

maxRows.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Default: 200. Automatically adds LIMIT to SELECT queries that don't already have one.

password.description: MySQL password. Passed securely via MYSQL_PWD env var (hidden from process list).

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_mysqlExecute
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a data-modifying SQL statement (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, ALTER, DROP) on a MySQL/MariaDB database via SSH. Requires user confirmation. SQL is piped via stdin. Only works on SSH/SFTP connections.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

sql.description: The SQL statement(s) to execute. Can contain multiple statements separated by semicolons.

password.description: MySQL password. Passed securely via MYSQL_PWD env var (hidden from process list).

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_mysqlSchema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspects MySQL/MariaDB database schema via SSH. Three modes: (1) No database/table — lists all databases. (2) Database only — lists tables with engine, row count, size. (3) Database + table — shows columns, CREATE TABLE, indexes. Only works on SSH/SFTP connections.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

database.description: Optional database name. If omitted, lists all databases. If provided without table, lists tables in this database.

table.description: Optional table name. When provided together with database, shows full table structure (columns, types, indexes, CREATE TABLE).

password.description: MySQL password. Passed securely via MYSQL_PWD env var (hidden from process list).

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_renameFile
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Renames or moves a file or directory on a remote server. Works with all protocols. Zero-data-transfer operation.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

oldPath.description: The current absolute path of the file or directory.

newPath.description: The new absolute path (rename or move destination).

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite the destination if it exists. Default: false.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_statFile
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Gets metadata about a file or directory on a remote server: type, size, modification time, permissions, and existence. Returns exists:false when the file does not exist. Works with all protocols.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The absolute remote path to get info about.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_copyFile
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Copies a file or directory on a remote server. On SSH copies server-side via 'cp -a' without network transfer. On FTP falls back to download+upload.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

sourcePath.description: The absolute path of the source file or directory.

destinationPath.description: The absolute path for the copy destination.

recursive.description: Whether to copy directories recursively. Default: true.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_findFiles
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Finds files and directories by name pattern (glob) on a remote server. On SSH uses 'find' server-side. Supports glob patterns (*.php, config*). Different from searchFiles which searches file CONTENTS — this searches file NAMES.

connectionName.description: Connection name. Auto-detected when only one connection is active.

path.description: The absolute remote directory to search in.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default: 50.

type.description: Filter results by type. 'file' returns only files, 'directory' returns only directories, 'any' (default) returns both.

excludePattern.description: Glob pattern to exclude from results (e.g. 'node_modules', '.git', 'vendor'). Matches against the full path.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_listConnections
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all saved remote connections with name, protocol, host, port, root path, and connection status.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_getChangedFiles
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns a summary of all remote files changed during the current session with badges: [M] modified, [A] added, [R] renamed, [D] deleted. Use after completing a task to review changes.

languageModelTools.name: remote-bridge_clearDecorations
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Clears session file change tracking and removes M/A/R/D badges from the Explorer.
